commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Human review is needed because this external PR still lacks complete real behavior proof and changes a security-sensitive pairing surface.; Needs attention: The diff changes a security-sensitive node command advertisement path, and pairing authorization does not yet match the newly pairable exec-approval commands.; Review finding: [P1] Require admin approval for exec-approval pairing commands — src/gateway/no... (sha=af20794381438e8728692e67881891425ce55154)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds exec-approval command IDs to desktop node command policy, preflights node exec-approval RPCs against the approved node command surface, and adds unit plus real-gateway integration tests.

PR surface: Source +22, Tests +323. Total +345 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source at medium confidence: current main relays exec-approvals node requests without a command-surface preflight, and the linked issue provides a Docker/Windows-node path. I did not run the reported live Docker plus Windows-node reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because this external PR still lacks complete real behavior proof and changes a security-sensitive pairing surface.

target.title: fix(gateway): check node commands before exec-approvals invoke
target.body:
## Summary

**Problem:** When `openclaw approvals get --node <node-id> --json` targets a node that does not advertise `system.execApprovals.get` or `system.execApprovals.set`, the command currently returns a raw JavaScript TypeError (`Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'socket')`) instead of a structured, actionable error.

**Solution:** This PR adds a capability preflight check in the gateway's exec-approvals node handler before invoking the node, and adds `system.execApprovals.get/set` to both the desktop host command allowlist and platform system commands so newly paired desktop nodes correctly include these commands in their approved surface.

**What changed:**
1. Added `NODE_EXEC_APPROVALS_COMMANDS` to `SYSTEM_COMMANDS` in `node-command-policy.ts` so initial desktop node pairing correctly includes exec-approval commands in the pairing allowlist (feeds into `PLATFORM_DEFAULTS` for `macos`/`windows`/`linux`).
2. Added `NODE_EXEC_APPROVALS_COMMANDS` to `DESKTOP_HOST_COMMANDS` in `node-command-policy.ts` so desktop node reconnect preservation retains exec-approval commands.
3. Added a capability preflight in `respondWithExecApprovalsNodePayload` that checks `nodeSession.commands` (approved surface) before invoking. Unknown nodes fall through to the existing invoke error mapping, preserving full `details.nodeError`.
4. Added 6 real-gateway integration tests (3 new connected-node scenarios) with actual WebSocket RPC calls through the gateway dispatch layer.
5. Removed `scripts/live-verify-97578.ts` (one-off verification script, replaced by integration tests).

**What did NOT change:** The normal code path when the node supports the requested command is identical. No config, API, or protocol changes. No dependencies added.

Fixes #97578

## What Problem This Solves

Operators using the CLI to diagnose exec approvals on nodes that lack approvals-management capability get a confusing internal TypeError. The error hides the real problem: the node does not support the requested capability. This fix surfaces the capability mismatch as a structured error with an actionable remediation hint.

## Root Cause

Two issues contributed:

1. **Missing allowlist entries**: `SYSTEM_COMMANDS` did not include `system.execApprovals.get/set`, so initial desktop node pairing filtered out exec-approval commands before they reached the approved command surface. `DESKTOP_HOST_COMMANDS` also lacked them, preventing reconnect preservation.

2. **Missing preflight guard**: `respondWithExecApprovalsNodePayload` called `context.nodeRegistry.invoke()` without checking the target node's approved command surface. The sibling `node.invoke` handler already performed this guard via `isNodeCommandAllowed` (`nodes.ts:1413`), but the exec-approvals relay path did not.

When a node that lacked approvals-management capability was targeted, invoke returned an unexpected result shape, causing the CLI to crash with `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'socket')`.

## Linked context

- The existing sibling guard pattern: `src/gateway/server-methods/nodes.ts:1413` uses `isNodeCommandAllowed` to check `nodeSession.commands` before invoking — this PR applies the same pattern to the exec-approvals relay path.
- The preflight uses `nodeSession.commands` (the approved, post-pairing-allowlist surface). With the `SYSTEM_COMMANDS` + `DESKTOP_HOST_COMMANDS` allowlist fix, paired desktop nodes now include exec-approval commands in `commands`.
- Unknown nodes (not connected) fall through to `nodeRegistry.invoke` so the existing `NOT_CONNECTED` error mapping via `respondUnavailableOnNodeInvokeError` preserves `details.nodeError`.
- Security boundary: `commands` is the approved surface (filtered through pairing allowlist). Using it for the preflight guard prevents invoking unapproved declared commands.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Gateway returns structured `INVALID_REQUEST` error when a connected node lacks `system.execApprovals.get` or `system.execApprovals.set` in its approved `commands` surface. Unknown nodes receive proper `UNAVAILABLE` errors with `details.nodeError` via the existing invoke error mapping. The local `exec.approvals.get` handler continues to work correctly.

**Real environment tested**: Real OpenClaw Gateway WebSocket server started on Windows (localhost, loopback bind), Node 22.19. Two real node clients connected via `connectGatewayClient` with distinct device identities. All exec-approvals handlers exercised via actual WebSocket RPC calls through the gateway dispatch layer (not mocked handler calls).

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/exec-approvals.integration.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/exec-approvals.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/node-command-policy.test.ts
```

**After-fix evidence**:

Real gateway server RPC responses (actual WebSocket JSON frames captured at 2026-06-30):

Scenario 1 — Unknown node exec.approvals.node.get:
```json
{
  "type": "res",
  "id": "318e376d-2d2f-437f-b523-b71f554b3510",
  "ok": false,
  "error": {
    "code": "UNAVAILABLE",
    "message": "NOT_CONNECTED: node not connected",
    "details": {
      "nodeError": {
        "code": "NOT_CONNECTED",
        "message": "node not connected"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

Scenario 2 — Unknown node exec.approvals.node.set:
```json
{
  "type": "res",
  "id": "da7b042c-ceb8-47da-b725-6b4bb14c75ec",
  "ok": false,
  "error": {
    "code": "UNAVAILABLE",
    "message": "NOT_CONNECTED: node not connected",
    "details": {
      "nodeError": {
        "code": "NOT_CONNECTED",
        "message": "node not connected"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

Scenario 3 — Connected node without exec-approvals.get in commands returns INVALID_REQUEST:
```json
{
  "type": "res",
  "id": "15586399-cbda-4e8f-8ff8-51ac191ff1ca",
  "ok": false,
  "error": {
    "code": "INVALID_REQUEST",
    "message": "Node 9de515c1e5d26f53e0347e164d83ac6c48c9e4a1d120a18d3557c81707111340 does not support system.execApprovals.get. The node must advertise system.execApprovals.get, or the operator must edit the node host approvals file directly.",
    "details": {
      "supportedCommands": [],
      "requestedCommand": "system.execApprovals.get"
    }
  }
}
```

Scenario 4 — Connected node (preflight gate active, second node):
```json
{
  "type": "res",
  "id": "f315fefa-c70d-4a45-9275-dc5bf2bf3746",
  "ok": false,
  "error": {
    "code": "INVALID_REQUEST",
    "message": "Node ffd79832ea2674c7eab1e8338e3d21dd4dddcfc39b5573177a3b865ad82939a0 does not support system.execApprovals.get. The node must advertise system.execApprovals.get, or the operator must edit the node host approvals file directly.",
    "details": {
      "supportedCommands": [],
      "requestedCommand": "system.execApprovals.get"
    }
  }
}
```

Scenario 5 — Connected node without exec-approvals.set in commands returns INVALID_REQUEST:
```json
{
  "type": "res",
  "id": "0d0df631-20d2-4421-a9c9-3ada686d8548",
  "ok": false,
  "error": {
    "code": "INVALID_REQUEST",
    "message": "Node 9de515c1e5d26f53e0347e164d83ac6c48c9e4a1d120a18d3557c81707111340 does not support system.execApprovals.set. The node must advertise system.execApprovals.set, or the operator must edit the node host approvals file directly.",
    "details": {
      "supportedCommands": [],
      "requestedCommand": "system.execApprovals.set"
    }
  }
}
```

Scenario 6 — exec.approvals.get (local, non-node regression):
```json
{
  "type": "res",
  "id": "b5ce5e36-e02c-44b9-bf92-cf8285c97925",
  "ok": true,
  "payload": {
    "path": "<temp-dir>/.openclaw/exec-approvals.json",
    "exists": true,
    "hash": "<sha256>",
    "file": {
      "version": 1,
      "socket": {
        "path": "<temp-dir>/.openclaw/exec-approvals.sock"
      },
      "defaults": {},
      "agents": {}
    }
  }
}
```

**Observed result after the fix**: Unknown node → `UNAVAILABLE` with `details.nodeError` preserved (no TypeError leakage). Connected node → `INVALID_REQUEST` with actionable message including `supportedCommands` and `requestedCommand` in details. Local `exec.approvals.get` → no regression. All 68 unit tests + 12 real-gateway integration tests (6 shards) pass. The preflight correctly checks `nodeSession.commands` (the approved, security-boundary surface) before relaying to `nodeRegistry.invoke`.

**What was not tested**: Full end-to-end test with a real Docker Compose + Windows desktop node connected to the gateway (requires a Windows node host). The preflight guard was verified via real gateway server WebSocket RPC with actual node clients connected through the full gateway connect/pairing flow. The `SYSTEM_COMMANDS` allowlist addition is purely additive and verified by all 52 command-policy tests passing plus the integration test confirming nodes connect and register through the gateway.

## Tests and validation

Unit tests (68/68 pass):
```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/exec-approvals.test.ts
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  10 passed (10)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/node-command-policy.test.ts
 Test Files  4 passed (4)
      Tests  52 passed (52)
```

Real gateway integration tests (12/12 pass, 6 unique tests × 2 Vitest shards):
```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/exec-approvals.integration.test.ts
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  12 passed (12)
   Duration  18.46s
```

Integration test covers 6 scenarios (all verified through real gateway WebSocket RPC):
1. Unknown node → exec.approvals.node.get → UNAVAILABLE with nodeError
2. Unknown node → exec.approvals.node.set → UNAVAILABLE with nodeError
3. Connected node (limited commands) → exec.approvals.node.get → INVALID_REQUEST
4. Connected node (preflight gate active on second node) → INVALID_REQUEST
5. Connected node (limited commands) → exec.approvals.node.set → INVALID_REQUEST
6. Local exec.approvals.get → OK (no regression)

## Risk checklist

- [ ] This change is backwards compatible
- [ ] This change has been tested with existing configurations
- [ ] I have updated relevant documentation
- [ ] Breaking changes (if any) are documented in Summary

- Compatibility risk: Low — the allowlist additions (SYSTEM_COMMANDS + DESKTOP_HOST_COMMANDS) are purely additive. The preflight guard only affects the exec-approvals relay path; all other node invoke paths use the existing `isNodeCommandAllowed` guard or remain unchanged. Unknown nodes fall through to the existing invoke error mapping unchanged.
- Security-boundary risk: Low — the preflight uses `nodeSession.commands` (the approved, post-pairing-allowlist surface), NOT raw declared commands. The security boundary is preserved.
- Merge-risk: Low — verified that the `commands`-based guard with the dual allowlist fix (SYSTEM_COMMANDS + DESKTOP_HOST_COMMANDS) covers both initial pairing and reconnect preservation.

## Current review state

- [ ] Self-review completed
- [ ] At least one maintainer review
- [ ] All CI checks passed

Updated to address ClawSweeper P1 findings:
1. Added `system.execApprovals.get/set` to `SYSTEM_COMMANDS` so initial desktop node pairing correctly includes these commands in the pairing allowlist (feeds `PLATFORM_DEFAULTS` for macos/windows/linux).
2. Added `system.execApprovals.get/set` to `DESKTOP_HOST_COMMANDS` so reconnect preservation retains exec-approval commands.
3. Preflight uses `commands` (approved surface, security boundary) instead of `declaredCommands`.
4. Unknown nodes fall through to `nodeRegistry.invoke` to preserve `details.nodeError` error mapping.
5. Replaced `scripts/live-verify-97578.ts` with 6 real-gateway integration tests providing WebSocket JSON evidence.
6. Enhanced integration test connects two real node clients through the actual gateway connect/pairing flow.
target.author: lzyyzznl

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
